# Test fixtures

These fixtures provide stable, minimal repositories for M1 parity testing:
the Rust `decon` dry-run output will be compared against the parity baseline
recorded here.

## Layout

| Fixture | Description | Module shape |
|---|---|---|
| `python-lib/` | Tiny single-package Python library | `src/` package + root config |
| `umbrella/` | Tiny Elixir-style umbrella monorepo | `apps/<name>/` (3 apps) + root config |
| `js-lib/` | Tiny TypeScript npm package | `src/` package + root config |

## Baseline

`baseline.json` records file counts, module inventory, filter statistics, and
setup documentation scores for each fixture. It is the **frozen parity oracle**
that M1 Rust tests must reproduce.

The baseline was originally generated by running the Python reference tools
(`crawl_local_files`, `monorepo_scope`, `assess_setup_docs`) against each
fixture. It is now maintained by a pure-Rust regenerator that reimplements
those heuristics as documented in `docs/best-practices.md`, so that no Python
toolchain is needed to verify or regenerate the baseline.

### Regenerating the baseline

`regenerate_baseline.rs` is a standalone, zero-dependency Rust file:

```bash
# Compile (no Cargo needed — pure std):
rustc tests/fixtures/regenerate_baseline.rs -o /tmp/regen_baseline

# Verify baseline.json matches the fixtures (exit 0 = match, 1 = mismatch):
/tmp/regen_baseline tests/fixtures/ --check

# Overwrite baseline.json after an intentional fixture change:
/tmp/regen_baseline tests/fixtures/ --write
```

CI runs `--check` on every push to guard against accidental fixture drift.

### How the regenerator relates to M1

The regenerator is a **minimal, standalone reimplementation** of the reference
heuristics — it is NOT `decon-crawl`. When `decon-crawl` is built in M1, it is
tested against the same frozen `baseline.json`, keeping the parity test
non-circular: two independent implementations must agree on the same oracle.

## Keeping fixtures stable

Do not add incidental files (editor configs, lockfiles, generated artifacts) to
fixtures. Every file here is intentional and contributes to the expected
baseline. If a fixture changes, regenerate `baseline.json` with `--write` and
update any snapshot expectations.
